text: Code review refresher because I keep seeing single-approval merges: Don't merge without 2 approvals unless it's a hotfix. Hotfixes need 1 approval + a follow-up review ticket filed within 24h.
does a rubber-stamp 'lgtm' count as an approval? asking for a friend :eyes:
if the diff is >100 lines and the review took 45 seconds, no. reviewers should at least run the code or trace the logic
what about docs-only changes? feels heavy to need 2 approvals to fix a typo in the README
docs-only and test-only changes: 1 approval is fine. the 2-approval rule is for anything that ships to prod
how do i mark something as a hotfix? just the label?
add the `hotfix` label AND prefix the PR title with [HOTFIX]. the merge bot checks the label, humans read the title
also please stop force-pushing over review comments, it nukes the thread context. append commits, squash at merge
staging is doing the thing again where the auth service returns 503 for ~2 min after every deploy. anyone else?
known issue, it's the connection pool warming up. see ACME-238. workaround: the smoke tests retry for 3 minutes before failing
ahh ok. it made me think my change broke auth, lost an hour to that :melting_face:
yeah it bites everyone once. real fix is lazy pool init, it's in the backlog
adding this to the onboarding doc so the next new person doesn't get got
good instinct chris :raised_hands:
proposal: run the smoke suite on every PR, not just pre-deploy. it's 90 seconds, would catch integration breaks way earlier
90s x 40 PRs a day is real CI money but honestly the June 9 incident would've been caught by exactly this
let's do it on PRs that touch api/ or services/, skip for frontend-only diffs
frontend can break the contract too... but ok as a start
done, shipped in ACME-252. path-filtered smoke on PRs is live
nice turnaround :fire:
